report_name: Immigration Law Platform DocketWise Discloses Breach Affecting Over 116,000 People

threat_actors.name: Unauthorized Actor
threat_actors.description: An unnamed threat actor who used valid credentials to access a third-party partner repository associated with DocketWise and exfiltrate sensitive personal information of 116,666 individuals.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for identity theft and fraud.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The attackers gained access to DocketWise data by compromising a trusted third-party partner, exploiting the supply chain relationship to access sensitive records.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The unauthorized actor used valid credentials to gain access to a third-party partner repository containing DocketWise customer data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The actor accessed and copied data from a third-party data repository containing law firm records managed by a partner of DocketWise.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The actor copied files containing sensitive records from the compromised third-party repository, constituting a data exfiltration event.

identities.name: DocketWise
identities.description: A cloud-based case management software provider for immigration law firms whose data was exposed via a third-party partner's repository.

identities.name: Immigration Law Clients
identities.description: A class of 116,666 individuals, clients of immigration law firms using DocketWise, whose sensitive personal information was exposed in the breach.

indicators.name: Anomalous Cloud Storage Data Egress
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Cloud Storage Data Egress
id: 4f8b1e2c-9d0a-4b7e-8c6d-1f3a9b8e0d2c
status: experimental
description: Detects unusually large data downloads from common cloud storage services, which could indicate data exfiltration as described in the DocketWise breach report. Thresholds should be tuned to the environment.
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/news/docketwise-data-breach-affects-116k-lawsuit-possible
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    sc-bytes: '>100000000' # Example threshold for large download (100MB)
    cs-host|contains:
      - 's3.amazonaws.com'
      - 'blob.core.windows.net'
      - 'storage.googleapis.com'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large data transfers, backups, or application updates.
level: medium
indicators.description: Monitors for anomalous access patterns from cloud storage repositories, such as unusually large data downloads, as recommended for detection in the article.
